title: Radial bitnet submission
body:
⛳ Experimental 16MB Submission: FRO + Radial Token Branch + 1024-Bucket Bigram Hash

This PR submits a public experimental entry for the Parameter Golf 16MB track.

The attached result was obtained from a verified development run on Kaggle Dual T4 hardware and is reported transparently as a development compressed-model result. No claim is made in this PR that the reported score has already been reproduced under the official 8xH100 SXM record-track setting.

Summary

This submission combines:

FRO (Fractal Resonant Optimization) as the main optimizer
a lightweight Radial Token Branch for analytic token-level geometric features
a direct 1024-bucket bigram hash branch for low-cost short-horizon lexical context
BitNet-style ternary-weight behavior in major internal projections
mixed post-training export (int8 / int6) with light export-time pruning

The goal of this design is to improve sample efficiency under a strict 10-minute training budget while remaining within the 16,000,000-byte artifact limit.

Configuration
Parameters: 18,476,354
Train sequence length: 1024
Batch size: 4
Development hardware: Kaggle Dual T4
Observed training wallclock: 600.2s
Audited artifact size: 15,943,179 bytes
Reported Result
Best observed val_bpb: 1.6130
Notes

This is the current best verified development result in the author’s FRO + radial token branch + bigram-hash branch line under the 16MB track constraint.

The repository entry includes:

README.md
submission.json
train.log
train_gpt.py
